handoff_brief.task_context: Migrate the eval infrastructure from 4 shell scripts with embedded Python (~87KB total: scripts/eval-run.sh, scripts/eval-graders.sh, scripts/eval-report.sh, scripts/eval-export-workbench.sh) to a TypeScript/Bun module system in evals/src/. The eval pipeline is the team's quality gate — it runs scenarios against AI agents, applies deterministic graders, scores via LLM rubric, and assembles results into JSON consumed by the web dashboard. There are 100+ scenarios across 7+ agents. Behavioral parity is critical: the web dashboard must not break, grader results must be identical, and the final JSON schema must not change.

domain_rules.rule: The pipeline executes 4 phases in strict order: Agents > Graders > Scoring > Assembly. No phase may start before its predecessor completes.
domain_rules.testable_assertion: Given phase='all', graders complete before scoring starts, scoring completes before assembly starts.

domain_rules.rule: Grader Override: if ANY deterministic grader fails, final score is forced to 'fail' regardless of LLM rubric score. Set grader_override=true in the scored result.
domain_rules.testable_assertion: Given a scenario where any grader returns passed=false, then final score='fail' and grader_override=true.

domain_rules.rule: Score is a closed enum of exactly 3 values: 'pass', 'partial', 'fail'. No normalization — 'Pass' (capitalized) is invalid, not silently lowercased.
domain_rules.testable_assertion: Given rubric returns 'Pass', treat as unknown/invalid (do NOT normalize to 'pass').

domain_rules.rule: All 8 grader types must be supported: json_valid, contains, not_contains, regex, section_present, field_count, length_bounds, json_field.
domain_rules.testable_assertion: Given scenarios using each grader type, results match the Python implementation exactly.

domain_rules.rule: contains/not_contains graders normalize text: lowercase + collapse underscores/hyphens/spaces to single space before comparison.
domain_rules.testable_assertion: Given contains with values=['business rules'] applied to 'Business_Rules', grader passes.

domain_rules.rule: json_field grader supports dot-path with [*] wildcard for array expansion and all check modes (type_check, min, max, contains, equals).
domain_rules.testable_assertion: Given path='business_rules.[*].invariant' and type_check='boolean', each array element's invariant field is validated as boolean.

domain_rules.rule: JSON extraction uses a 3-tier strategy: (1) direct JSON.parse, (2) strip markdown fences then parse, (3) brace-depth scan for first complete JSON object.
domain_rules.testable_assertion: Given output with JSON inside ```json fences, extraction succeeds via strategy 2.

domain_rules.rule: Trial 0 uses base filename '{agent}-{scenario_id}.json'. Trials 1+ use suffix '-t{N}.json'.
domain_rules.testable_assertion: Given --trials 3, files are: base.json, base-t1.json, base-t2.json.

domain_rules.rule: pass@k = true if at least 1 of k trials scored 'pass'. flaky = true if not all trial scores are identical.
domain_rules.testable_assertion: Given 3 trials with scores [fail, pass, fail], pass@3=true, flaky=true.

domain_rules.rule: Team scenarios execute phases sequentially. Human fixture phases (phase_N_agent='human') use the prompt text as output — no LLM call.
domain_rules.testable_assertion: Given team scenario with phase 2 as human, phase 2 output = phase 2 prompt text, no claude CLI invocation.

domain_rules.rule: NDJSON parsing: skip blank lines and non-JSON lines. Agent output comes from the LAST event with type='result', from its result field.
domain_rules.testable_assertion: Given NDJSON with multiple result events, agent_output is extracted from the final one.

domain_rules.rule: Resume: when --resume DIR is passed, skip scenarios whose raw output file already exists on disk.
domain_rules.testable_assertion: Given existing raw output for scenario-01, only scenario-02+ are executed.

domain_rules.rule: Baseline detection: is_complete_baseline=true only when scenarios_run >= 100 AND scenarios_run >= 0.8 * ALL_SCENARIOS_TOTAL.
domain_rules.testable_assertion: Given 15 scenarios run out of 120 total, is_complete_baseline=false.

domain_rules.rule: Scoring retries once on JSON parse failure. On total failure (both attempts), default to score='fail'.
domain_rules.testable_assertion: Given invalid JSON on first LLM response and valid on retry, second response is used.

domain_rules.rule: Timeouts: individual scenario=300s, team scenario=600s, overridable via --timeout-per-phase.
domain_rules.testable_assertion: Given no override, individual agent call times out at 300s.

domain_rules.rule: calibration_gap = avg_confidence - weighted_score, where weights are pass=100, partial=50, fail=0.
Scenario type derived from title: 'edge' in title -> edge-case, 'adversarial'/'attack' -> adversarial, 'regression' -> regression, default -> happy-path.
Graders block in scenario file: only match top-level 'graders:' line (no leading whitespace). Indented occurrences inside prompts must be ignored.
domain_rules.testable_assertion: Given 'graders:' indented inside a prompt block, it is NOT parsed as the graders section.

domain_rules.rule: Phase 4 (report) triggers DB migration via web/src/migrate.ts. Migration failure is non-fatal — run still succeeds and JSON is written.
domain_rules.testable_assertion: Given migration script fails, final JSON is still written and exit code is 0.

architecture_guidance.decision: Create evals/src/ directory with flat module system, one file per component
architecture_guidance.implementation_note: Files: types.ts, json-extract.ts, scenario-parser.ts, graders.ts, discovery.ts, concurrency.ts, claude-adapter.ts, agent-runner.ts, scorer.ts, assembler.ts, pipeline.ts, cli.ts, report.ts, workbench-export.ts. Tests in evals/src/__tests__/*.test.ts.

architecture_guidance.decision: Dependency Injection via ClaudeAdapter interface for testability
architecture_guidance.implementation_note: Define a ClaudeAdapter interface wrapping Bun.spawn. Production code injects real adapter; tests inject a mock that returns canned NDJSON. This is the only external I/O boundary that needs DI.
json-extract, scenario-parser, graders, and assembler are pure functions — zero I/O, fully unit-testable. agent-runner and scorer are impure (subprocess + filesystem). Keep the boundary explicit.
Map<string, GraderFn> where GraderFn = (grader: GraderDef, output: string) => GraderResult. Each of 8 types is a registered function. Easy to test individually.

architecture_guidance.decision: Promise.all + concurrency limiter for parallel execution
architecture_guidance.implementation_note: Use a simple semaphore/limiter (no external deps). Default concurrency = 10, overridable via --parallel N. Native to Bun, simpler than worker threads. Bottleneck is I/O (claude CLI calls), not CPU.

architecture_guidance.decision: Implementation order: inside-out, pure modules first
Use bun:test, not jest or vitest
architecture_guidance.implementation_note: Bun has a built-in test runner. No external test dependencies needed. Import from 'bun:test'.
Bun provides everything needed: subprocess (Bun.spawn), filesystem (Bun.file), test runner (bun:test). Do not add npm packages.

architecture_guidance.decision: Do NOT change scenario file format or final result JSON schema
architecture_guidance.implementation_note: 120+ scenario .md files exist. The web dashboard (web/src/) consumes the final JSON. Both are frozen interfaces. The migration is an internal refactor — external contracts must not change.

architecture_guidance.decision: Test scenario parser against REAL scenario files on disk
architecture_guidance.implementation_note: Do not rely solely on synthetic test fixtures. Parse actual evals/*/scenario-*.md files to catch edge cases in the markdown format.

acceptance_criteria.when: TS runner discovers scenarios with same filters (--agent, comma-separated, qualified agent/scenario-id)
acceptance_criteria.then: Identical scenario set discovered as Python implementation

acceptance_criteria.criterion: All 8 grader types produce identical results
acceptance_criteria.given: Scenario with graders of each type applied to known output
acceptance_criteria.then: Same pass/fail and detail messages as Python for all 8 types

acceptance_criteria.given: Agent output contains JSON in markdown fences

acceptance_criteria.when: Applied to output with array of objects
acceptance_criteria.then: Each element's invariant field validated as boolean

acceptance_criteria.given: Any grader fails for a scenario
acceptance_criteria.then: Final score forced to 'fail', grader_override=true in result
3 agent calls made, trial 0 = base filename, trials 1-2 = -t1/-t2 suffix, pass@k and flaky computed

acceptance_criteria.given: Previous raw output directory with some results
acceptance_criteria.then: Only missing scenarios executed, existing outputs untouched

acceptance_criteria.given: Team scenario with 3 phases (agent, human, agent)
acceptance_criteria.then: Phase 1: LLM call, Phase 2: fixture (prompt as output), Phase 3: LLM call. Sequential. Per-phase graders applied.

acceptance_criteria.given: Filtered run with fewer scenarios than threshold

acceptance_criteria.then: Schema matches Python output exactly: run_id, date, is_complete_baseline, scenarios_total, scenarios_run, summary, results, agent_summaries, meta
Blank/non-JSON lines skipped, agent_output extracted from last result event
Grader passes (underscores normalized to spaces, case folded)

acceptance_criteria.given: LLM returns invalid JSON on first attempt
acceptance_criteria.then: Valid second attempt is used; total failure defaults to score='fail'
Listed with RUN/SKIP status, zero agent calls, zero files created

acceptance_criteria.given: Grader module imported in unit test
acceptance_criteria.then: All grader behaviors verified without LLM calls or subprocess

terminology_alignment.definition: A markdown test case file containing prompt, expected_behavior, failure_modes, scoring_rubric, optional graders, and metadata.
When any deterministic grader fails, this flag is set to true and final score is forced to 'fail' regardless of LLM rubric result.
Phase 1 artifact: JSON containing agent_output, trace, tokens, cost, duration, metadata.
Phase 3 artifact: JSON with final score, grader results, rubric details, trial data.
Phase 4 artifact: single JSON file with full run report consumed by web dashboard.
A team scenario phase where no LLM is called; the prompt text becomes the phase output directly.
True when scenarios_run >= 100 AND >= 80% of total available scenarios.
avg_confidence - weighted_score (pass=100, partial=50, fail=0). Positive = overconfident.

high_confidence_areas: All 8 grader types and their exact behavior — verified against source code
Pipeline phase ordering — confirmed in eval-run.sh
Trial naming convention — confirmed in eval-run.sh
Scenario file format and discovery — confirmed by glob of 100+ files
Architecture and implementation order — MJ's design is sound and consistent with Bird's analysis
low_confidence_areas: Exact normalization regex in Python contains/not_contains — should be verified character-by-character when implementing
json_field [*] wildcard edge cases on non-array values — need to port Python logic carefully
web/src/sse.ts integration — may need updates if it currently shells out to eval scripts
assumptions: Bun runtime is already available in the project environment
The web dashboard consumes only the final JSON file, not intermediate artifacts
No other scripts or CI pipelines depend on the shell script interfaces directly
